attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Adverse cutaneous drug reaction (candidate — severe drug eruption differential)

transform_flags: Age inferred as adult (~28) — the source note states 'young adult' with no exact age; body weight 68 kg and independent functional status establish an adult. Please confirm on clinician review.

Sex inferred as female with NO firm basis in the source note (nothing sex-specific is stated) — recorded only because the schema requires sex_at_birth; flag for clinician confirmation/correction. Does not affect the diagnosis, tier or scoring.
Occupation not stated in the source ('not stated' recorded); living_situation ('lives independently') inferred from independent functional status — flagged as inference.
Geographic context not stated in the source — omitted rather than guessed.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician who inspected/characterised the eruption (a 4 cm oval herald plaque with a peripheral collarette of scale on the left flank, secondary 1-2 cm salmon oval papules/plaques with a fine inward-facing trailing collarette, long axes along the cleavage lines in a 'Christmas tree' pattern, clear face/palms/soles, no mucosal involvement, no lymphadenopathy), measured vitals and reconciled medications against the EHR. Patient/video-observable findings (the herald patch and widespread oval scaly spots, the cleavage-line pattern, sparing of face/palms/soles, general well appearance/anxiety, patient impression of no fever, the recent viral prodrome) routed to 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false, source-channel tagged); clinician-measured vitals (BP 122/78, HR 85, Temp 36.9, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Wt 68 kg), palpation for lymphadenopathy and syphilis serology sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits. The clinician's in-person reassurance and symptomatic advice reprojected to patient-performed measures; OTC hydrocortisone 1% and cetirizine advised directly, with routine GP review arranged.

De-anchoring applied: the source filename/header name the diagnosis (pityriasis rosea). The green side (01/02) carries only the lay presentation (a big scaly patch first, then hundreds of small oval itchy spots across the trunk, lining up along the skin creases, sparing face/palms/soles, after a mild cold ~10 days ago, no new medicines, systemically well) and no diagnosis label. The full primary_diagnosis.name does not appear in any injectable field.

Tier set to T1 (self-care/reassurance with routine GP review), NOT an urgent/emergency tier: pityriasis rosea is a benign, non-contagious, self-limiting post-viral eruption, the source's own Risk Level is 'Low' with a negative red-flag screen, and its disposition was reassurance + OTC symptomatic treatment with routine GP review in 2-3 weeks. Management is patient-performable/OTC (mild topical corticosteroid, oral antihistamine, lukewarm showers, avoiding heat/harsh soaps) so the baseline stays T1. The alarming explosive onset and un-examinable mimics drive prominent mandatory safety-net edges (T2 secondary-syphilis screen + serology on palm/sole/mucosal involvement; T4 systemic illness/fever; T4 blistering/peeling/skin pain / SJS-TEN concern) — not a baseline uplift. A prompt T2 review to arrange syphilis serology is an acceptable more-cautious disposition; reflexive escalation to 000/ED (T3+ as the baseline) and pure reassurance with no safety-net (T0) are both scored as errors (over- and under-triage).
Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00031 — maintainer to confirm/reassign the SEQ within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.

Candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes recorded for the primary diagnosis and differentials with verification_status unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; none claimed verified. The ICD-10-AM code (L42, Pityriasis rosea) and the SNOMED differentials are best-effort candidates for later terminology-receipt confirmation.
Drug AMT/PBS codes for hydrocortisone 1% cream and cetirizine were NOT assigned (candidate pending terminology receipt) rather than fabricated; amt_snomed_code omitted on the medication objects.

Doses (hydrocortisone 1% cream twice daily to the itchiest spots; cetirizine 10 mg once daily) taken verbatim from the source note; none synthesised. Adult patient — no paediatric dosing issue. A topical corticosteroid is appropriate here (inflammatory, non-infective eruption), in contrast to infective dermatoses where it is contraindicated.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
